metadata.description: virtualenv: `virtualenv` creates isolated environments. It was created before `venv`, supporting Python 2 and allows access to the global site packages (`--system-site-packages`). Here is a brief example of creating an environment with `virtualenv`: ```python # Install virtualenv if not already installed pip install virtualenv # Create a new virtual environment named 'myenv' with global site packages virtualenv --system-site-packages myenv # Activate the virtual environment on Unix or MacOS source myenv/bin/activate # Or activate it on Windows myenv\Scripts\activate ``` Note: The above example is provided for illustrative purposes and assumes you are using a Unix-like shell or PowerShell on Windows.
